skill.name: using-git-worktrees
skill.description: Creates isolated Git worktrees for feature work, with directory selection, .gitignore checks, setup commands, and baseline test verification.
skill.summary: Set up isolated Git worktrees before implementation work.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell and filesystem patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no prompt injection, malware, network access, secret collection, or real cryptographic weakness, but the workflow does instruct agents to run local setup, build, and test commands that can execute project code.

medium_findings.title: Project Setup Commands Can Execute Local Code
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs agents to run dependency installation, build, and test commands. This is legitimate for repository setup, but these commands can execute scripts from the target project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly lists package-manager, build, and test commands. The risk depends on the repository being trusted, so this is a legitimate workflow with meaningful local execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Workflow May Modify Repository Ignore Rules
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to add a worktree directory to .gitignore and commit the change when the ignore rule is missing. This is not malicious, but it changes repository state as part of setup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to edit and commit .gitignore is explicit. It is operationally reasonable, but marketplace users should know the skill can alter tracked files.

low_findings.title: Hidden Directory Findings Are Benign Worktree Paths
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hidden and home-directory paths. Review shows these are suggested worktree storage locations and not evidence of secret harvesting or hidden persistence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced paths are shown in user-facing setup examples. No code reads credentials, hides payloads, or exfiltrates data from those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the reviewed lines contain skill metadata and integration text. No cryptographic operation is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is no encryption, hashing, or cryptographic API usage in SKILL.md. The static result appears to be incidental text matching.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell snippets for directory checks, Git worktree creation, package installation, builds, and tests. These commands are expected for the skill purpose but require trusted repository context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are plainly visible and central to the skill. No malicious intent is present, but command execution is a real operational risk.

content.value_statement: Feature work can disrupt a busy repository when branch switching changes the active workspace. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through isolated Git worktree setup with safety checks.
actual_capabilities: Chooses an existing .worktrees or worktrees directory when present.
Checks CLAUDE.md for project-specific worktree directory preferences.
Prompts the user when no directory preference can be found.
Verifies .gitignore before using project-local worktree directories.
Creates a new Git worktree and branch for isolated feature work.
Runs detected setup and baseline test commands for common project types.
limitations: Requires a Git repository that supports worktrees.
Runs local project commands, which should only happen in trusted repositories.
Does not define cleanup after implementation work is complete.
May need user guidance for projects with custom setup or test commands.

use_cases.title: Start Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Create an isolated branch workspace before a coding agent begins a new feature.

use_cases.title: Protect Current Work
use_cases.description: Keep uncommitted changes in the main workspace separate from experimental implementation work.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Setup
use_cases.description: Give coding assistants a repeatable process for directory selection, ignore checks, and baseline tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Worktree
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new feature branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated worktree for my next feature. Ask before choosing a location if the project has no preference.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Set up before executing a plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before implementing this plan, use the using-git-worktrees skill. Verify the worktree directory, create the branch, install dependencies if needed, and report the baseline test result.

prompt_templates.title: Respect Project Conventions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the using-git-worktrees skill and follow this repository's existing conventions. Check for .worktrees, worktrees, and CLAUDE.md before asking me where to create the worktree.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Worktree Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the using-git-worktrees skill to prepare a worktree for parallel development. Confirm .gitignore safety, create the branch, run detected setup, run baseline tests, and summarize any blockers.

output_examples.input: Set up a worktree for an authentication feature.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports the selected worktree directory, confirms the ignore rule, creates the feature branch workspace, runs setup, and states whether tests passed.

output_examples.input: Prepare an isolated workspace when no worktree directory exists.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks the user to choose between a project-local hidden directory and a global worktree location before creating anything.

output_examples.input: Run baseline checks after creating the worktree.
output_examples.output: The assistant detects the project type, runs the matching setup and test workflow, then reports failures or readiness for implementation.

best_practices: Use the skill only inside repositories you trust.
Review any .gitignore change before committing it.
Treat failing baseline tests as pre-existing blockers unless the user approves continuing.
anti_patterns: Do not create project-local worktrees before checking .gitignore.
Do not assume the worktree directory when project conventions are unclear.
Do not run setup or test commands in an untrusted repository.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create isolated Git worktrees for feature work or implementation tasks.

faq.question: Does it choose a worktree directory automatically?
faq.answer: It uses existing project directories first, then checks CLAUDE.md, then asks the user.

faq.question: Why does it check .gitignore?
faq.answer: The check helps prevent project-local worktree contents from being accidentally tracked by Git.

faq.question: Can it run project commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It may run dependency installation, build, and test commands when project files indicate them.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: No. Setup and test commands can execute repository-controlled code, so use trusted repositories only.

faq.question: Does it clean up finished worktrees?
faq.answer: No. The skill references a separate finishing workflow for cleanup after development is complete.
